# Upbit.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with Upbit.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, or anti-hallucination rules.

Upbit.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for Upbit public REST and WebSocket quotation APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, dependency injection, and socket patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 3.2.0.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 12.2.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, feature table, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Upbit.Net/
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `UpbitRestClient` for REST and `UpbitSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to Upbit endpoints.
- Do not configure credentials. Current Upbit.Net does not expose authenticated endpoints and credentials are not part of the options surface.
- Do not generate order placement, order querying, balances, deposits, withdrawals, private streams, margin, or futures code. This library exposes public Spot quotation data only.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- Direct REST and SharedApis REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`.
- Direct and SharedApis WebSocket subscription methods return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`.
- SharedApis helper methods that do not perform HTTP/socket I/O can return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate a REST or socket client per request in production code.
- Prefer dependency injection for long-running services.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Do not call `.Result` or `.Wait()` on async methods.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`.
- Use Upbit symbol format `QUOTE-BASE`, for example `USDT-ETH` or `KRW-BTC`.
- For multi-exchange market-data code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` interfaces from `.SharedClient`.
- Use `SharedClient.Discover()` when tooling or exchange-agnostic code needs runtime metadata about implemented shared interfaces and endpoint options.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package JKorf.Upbit.Net
```

## Required Usings

Typical REST code:

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
using Upbit.Net.Enums;
```

Environment setup:

```csharp
using Upbit.Net;
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Retry/error helper code may also need:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Upbit.Net;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data:

```csharp
var client = new UpbitRestClient();

var ticker = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

Socket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new UpbitSocketClient();
```

Regional environments:

```csharp
var korea = new UpbitRestClient(options => options.Environment = UpbitEnvironment.Live);
var singapore = new UpbitRestClient(options => options.Environment = UpbitEnvironment.Singapore);
var indonesia = new UpbitRestClient(options => options.Environment = UpbitEnvironment.Indonesia);
var thailand = new UpbitRestClient(options => options.Environment = UpbitEnvironment.Thailand);
```

Available environment names are exposed by `UpbitEnvironment.All`. `UpbitEnvironment.CreateCustom(...)` can create a custom REST/socket address pair.

## Dependency Injection

Current DI shape:

```csharp
services.AddUpbit(options =>
{
    options.Environment = UpbitEnvironment.Singapore;
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
public class MyService
{
    private readonly IUpbitRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly IUpbitSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(IUpbitRestClient restClient, IUpbitSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

Interface namespaces:

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Interfaces.Clients;
```

DI also registers shared interfaces, `IUpbitOrderBookFactory`, `IUpbitTrackerFactory`, and the base `ITrackerFactory`.

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var price = result.Data.LastPrice;
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<HttpResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<HttpResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    HttpResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
UpbitRestClient
  .SpotApi
    .ExchangeData
      .GetSymbolsAsync(...)
      .GetTickerAsync(...)
      .GetTickersAsync(...)
      .GetTickersByQuoteAssetsAsync(...)
      .GetTradeHistoryAsync(...)
      .GetOrderBookAsync(...)
      .GetOrderBooksAsync(...)
      .GetKlinesAsync(...)
      .GetSymbolConfigAsync(...)
    .SharedClient
```

WebSocket:

```text
UpbitSocketClient
  .SpotApi
    .SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(...)
    .SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(...)
    .SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(...)
    .SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(...)
    .SharedClient
```

Important: there is no `SpotApi.Account`, `SpotApi.Trading`, futures API, margin API, wallet API, or authenticated user data stream in this library.

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common REST tasks:

```text
Get supported symbols -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(includeNotifications: true)
Get one ticker -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH")
Get multiple tickers -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(new[] { "USDT-ETH", "USDT-BTC" })
Get all tickers for quote assets -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersByQuoteAssetsAsync(new[] { "USDT", "BTC" })
Get recent trades -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTradeHistoryAsync("USDT-ETH", limit: 100)
Get order book -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("USDT-ETH", levels: 15)
Get multiple order books -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBooksAsync(new[] { "USDT-ETH", "USDT-BTC" }, levels: 15)
Get klines/candles -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("USDT-ETH", KlineInterval.OneMinute, limit: 100)
Get symbol config -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolConfigAsync("USDT-ETH")
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Subscribe ticker -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync("USDT-ETH", handler)
Subscribe many tickers -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(new[] { "USDT-ETH", "USDT-BTC" }, handler)
Subscribe trades -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync("USDT-ETH", handler)
Subscribe order book -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync("USDT-ETH", 15, handler)
Subscribe klines -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync("USDT-ETH", KlineInterval.OneMinute, handler)
```

## Spot Market Data Example

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
using Upbit.Net.Enums;

var client = new UpbitRestClient();

var ticker = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine($"USDT-ETH last price: {ticker.Data.LastPrice}");

var candles = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync(
    "USDT-ETH",
    KlineInterval.OneMinute,
    limit: 10);

if (!candles.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(candles.Error);
    return;
}

foreach (var candle in candles.Data)
    Console.WriteLine($"{candle.OpenTime:u} O={candle.OpenPrice} H={candle.HighPrice} L={candle.LowPrice} C={candle.ClosePrice}");
```

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
using Upbit.Net.Enums;

var socketClient = new UpbitSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    "USDT-ETH",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

var klineSub = await socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(
    "USDT-ETH",
    KlineInterval.OneMinute,
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.ClosePrice));

if (!klineSub.Success)
{
    await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
    Console.WriteLine(klineSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(klineSub.Data);
```

Upbit socket options contain `SubscribeMaxWaitForError`, because the server replies only when a subscription has an error and a subscription is assumed successful after the configured wait.

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

Use CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis when the user asks for code that can work against Upbit and other exchanges.

```csharp
using Upbit.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;

ISpotTickerRestClient tickerClient = new UpbitRestClient().SpotApi.SharedClient;
var symbol = new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT");

var ticker = await tickerClient.GetSpotTickerAsync(new GetTickerRequest(symbol));
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

`ISpotSymbolRestClient.GetSpotSymbolsAsync(...)` honors the filters in `GetSymbolsRequest`. Returned `SharedSpotSymbol` values include `DisplayName`, a crypto `BaseAssetType`, and quote classification: `KRW`, `SGD`, `IDR`, and `THB` are fiat; recognized stablecoins use `SharedAssetSubType.StableCoin`; other quotes are crypto. The interface's `SpotSymbolCatalog` property exposes the cached catalog for the active environment.

Shared WebSocket subscriptions return an `UpdateSubscription` in `.Data`. For shared interface variables, unsubscribe through the concrete socket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new UpbitSocketClient();
ITickerSocketClient tickerSocket = socketClient.SpotApi.SharedClient;

var sub = await tickerSocket.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    new SubscribeTickerRequest(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT")),
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Do not call `tickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(...)` on `ITickerSocketClient`; that shared interface does not expose the method.

## Symbol And Region Handling

Upbit native symbol format is `QUOTE-BASE`.

```text
Korea live examples:      KRW-BTC, KRW-ETH, BTC-ETH, USDT-BTC
Singapore live examples:  SGD-BTC, SGD-ETH, BTC-ETH, USDT-BTC
Indonesia live examples:  IDR-BTC, IDR-ETH, BTC-ETH, USDT-BTC
Thailand live examples:   THB-BTC, THB-ETH, BTC-ETH, USDT-BTC
```

Do not use Binance-style concatenated symbols such as `ETHUSDT`. For SharedApis, use `new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT")`; the library formats the symbol as `USDT-ETH`.

For all tickers, use quote assets that fit the selected environment:

```csharp
var koreaTickers = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersByQuoteAssetsAsync(new[] { "KRW", "BTC", "USDT" });
```

The shared REST client uses environment-aware quote assets for `GetSpotTickersAsync`: Korea uses `KRW`, Singapore `SGD`, Indonesia `IDR`, Thailand `THB`, plus `BTC` and `USDT`.

## Local Order Book

Use `IUpbitOrderBookFactory` for maintained local order books:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
using Upbit.Net.Interfaces;

var services = new ServiceCollection();
services.AddUpbit();
var provider = services.BuildServiceProvider();

var factory = provider.GetRequiredService<IUpbitOrderBookFactory>();
var book = factory.Create(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT"));
var started = await book.StartAsync();
if (!started.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(started.Error);
    return;
}

var snapshot = book.Book;
```

## Error Handling Notes

Upbit.Net returns API/network/rate-limit errors through `.Error` on result objects. It generally does not throw for normal API failures. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

Common Upbit.Net error categories:

```text
Network or server errors: usually transient; retry with backoff if result.Error.IsTransient is true.
Rate limiting: transient; the library has client-side rate limiting, but server-side limits can still be hit.
Invalid symbol: permanent; fix symbol format and verify with GetSymbolsAsync(true).
Invalid interval: permanent; use Upbit.Net.Enums.KlineInterval values.
Unsupported authenticated feature: permanent; this library does not expose private APIs.
Wrong environment/quote asset: permanent until the quote asset or environment is changed.
```

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs
  Client setup, public ticker, supported symbols, candles, order book, success checks.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-market-data.cs
  Symbols, regional quote assets, tickers, trades, klines, order books, symbol config.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Public ticker stream, trade stream, order book stream, kline stream, success checks, teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis REST and socket market-data patterns.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  HttpResult pattern, transient retry, Upbit-specific mistakes, symbol validation.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package JKorf.Upbit.Net`.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

Before finalizing generated code:

- Does it compile with the current package?
- Does it use `UpbitRestClient` / `UpbitSocketClient`?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does it avoid raw Upbit URLs and manual JSON parsing?
- Does it avoid credentials and private API code?
- Does it avoid nonexistent `SpotApi.Trading`, `SpotApi.Account`, futures, margin, wallet, and user-stream branches?
- Does it use Upbit `QUOTE-BASE` symbols or `SharedSymbol` for shared code?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?
- Does the environment match the quote asset being requested?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IUpbitRestClient.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IUpbitSocketClient.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IUpbitRestClientSpotApi.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IUpbitRestClientSpotApiExchangeData.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IUpbitSocketClientSpotApi.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IUpbitRestClientSpotApiShared.cs
Upbit.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/IUpbitSocketClientSpotApiShared.cs
Upbit.Net/UpbitEnvironment.cs
Upbit.Net/Objects/Options/UpbitRestOptions.cs
Upbit.Net/Objects/Options/UpbitSocketOptions.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new ApiCredentials("key", "secret");
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new UpbitRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...);
```

Use:

```csharp
// Upbit.Net does not expose order placement.
var ticker = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("ETHUSDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("USDT-ETH");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await sharedTickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Use:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```
